raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation asks for help with a research section whose thesis, object of evaluation, and contribution framing may need reframing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The section contains an argument connecting ambiguity, interpretation alignment, benchmark construction, accuracy results, and alignment conclusions, so logical consistency is centrally implicated.","R1.3":"The artifact makes claims about user-intent alignment based on benchmark labels and top-1 accuracy, so evidence-claim alignment is a central responsibility.","R1.5":"Because the request targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are expected parts of the help.","R1.6":"General help with a draft section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about the user’s exact desired intervention because the delegation is broad.","RX.2":"The projection must account for possible overclaims, especially around user intent and first-method novelty.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing edits or review of the artifact.","RX.4":"The section concerns system alignment with user intent, so avoiding downstream harm from overstated or misleading claims remains active.","RX.5":"The projection should remain traceable to features explicitly present in the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks for help with a research section whose thesis, object of evaluation, and contribution framing may need reframing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The section contains an argument connecting ambiguity, interpretation alignment, benchmark construction, accuracy results, and alignment conclusions, so logical consistency is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes claims about user-intent alignment based on benchmark labels and top-1 accuracy, so evidence-claim alignment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are expected parts of the help.
rationale.R1.6: General help with a draft section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about the user’s exact desired intervention because the delegation is broad.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for possible overclaims, especially around user intent and first-method novelty.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than performing edits or review of the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The section concerns system alignment with user intent, so avoiding downstream harm from overstated or misleading claims remains active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to features explicitly present in the delegation and artifact.
